title: Tech Hiring Slowdown and Selective Recovery
summary: Major tech companies reduced hiring by 30-50% from 2023 peaks. Recovery is underway but selective -- AI/ML roles and infrastructure engineering see strong demand while general SWE roles remain competitive. Startups absorbing displaced talent from FAANG layoffs.

title: Healthcare Hiring Surge Continues
summary: Healthcare sector experiencing sustained hiring growth driven by aging population, post-pandemic capacity expansion, and mental health investment. Nursing shortages particularly acute in rural areas. Travel nursing rates stabilizing after pandemic peak but still elevated.

title: Remote Work Normalization and Hybrid Dominance
summary: Remote-first job postings stabilized at 15-20% of total (down from 25% peak). Hybrid (2-3 days in office) emerged as dominant model at 45% of postings. Companies offering full remote see 2.5x more applications but face retention challenges. Geographic pay differentials narrowing.

title: AI/ML Talent Demand Spike
summary: Demand for AI/ML engineers, prompt engineers, and LLM specialists surged 40%+ YoY. Companies across all sectors building AI capabilities. Salaries for senior AI roles exceed $300K in major metros. Supply gap estimated at 3:1 (openings vs qualified candidates).

title: Job Board CPC Inflation Continues
summary: Average cost-per-click on major job boards increased 12-18% YoY as competition intensifies. Indeed and LinkedIn lead price increases. Programmatic buying provides 15-25% savings vs direct but requires minimum $10K/month spend. Emerging boards (Otta, Wellfound) offer lower CPCs for tech roles.

title: DEI Hiring Practices Evolution
summary: Corporate DEI hiring programs undergoing transformation amid legal challenges. Focus shifting from quota-based approaches to skills-based hiring that naturally broadens candidate pools. Blind resume screening adoption up 35%. Pay equity audits now standard at Fortune 500 companies.

title: Gig and Contingent Workforce Expansion
summary: Contingent workforce now represents 35-40% of total workforce at large enterprises. Platforms like Upwork, Toptal, and Fiverr see 20% revenue growth. Companies building dedicated contingent workforce programs. Regulatory scrutiny on worker classification intensifying globally.

title: Skills-Based Hiring Replacing Degree Requirements
summary: Major employers (Google, Apple, IBM, GM) removing degree requirements for most roles. 45% of job postings now list skills instead of degrees. Assessment-based hiring tools growing 25% YoY. Bootcamp and certification acceptance increasing as alternatives to traditional degrees.
